name: Elves Stage
description: Prepare a long-running Elves session without starting risky implementation work.

fields.name: validation_commands
fields.description: Commands that prove the run is safe

steps.name: orient
steps.prompt:
Orient for an Elves-style long-running session.

Read the run goal, repo state, active branch, tests, PR state, existing docs, and risks. Identify exact batches that can be completed independently. Write the orientation to `docs/elves/execution-log.md` and `.elves-session.json`.

steps.name: contract
steps.prompt:
Write the durable memory contract for the Elves run.

Create or refresh `docs/elves/survival-guide.md`, `docs/elves/learnings.md`, `docs/elves/execution-log.md`, and `.elves-session.json`. Include goal, non-goals, stop rules, validation commands, branch/PR details, batch list, current risks, and how to resume after sleep or context loss.

steps.name: preflight
steps.prompt:
Run Elves preflight.

Verify clean or intentionally dirty git state, remote/auth, base branch, validation commands, PR status if any, sleep/session readiness, collision risks, and whether unattended work is appropriate. Record exact readiness and blockers in `docs/elves/execution-log.md`.

steps.name: stage
steps.prompt:
Stop for staging review.

Summarize the launch command, first batch, stop conditions, validation gates, and known risks. Do not begin implementation in this ticket. This ticket is successful when the run is ready for an ELVBATCH launch.

on_reject.prompt:
The Elves stage review requested changes: {{ rejection_reason }}

Revise the run contract and preflight before asking for launch approval again.
